summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

actions.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that blocks PRD readiness when full-tool Claude agents mutate target project files or lack required evidence scope declarations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots create file digest maps before/after agent execution to detect mutations.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit implementation detects project file changes.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot taken before agents, diff after execution, violations generate blockers that prevent PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement of boundary audit blocks mutations.

evidence.summary: Execution policy explicitly declares project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots as artifact directory only.
evidence.why: Policy documents the boundary contract enforced by the audit.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules forbid modifying target project files and require evidence scope declarations.
evidence.why: Evidence agents are instructed not to mutate projects.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set restricts evidence to project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Evidence scope validation prevents unverified claims.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence by scope and source type, ensuring project facts require project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Classification enforces evidence requirements for existing project claims.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with concrete locators.
evidence.why: Enforcement prevents assumptions from becoming facts.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues generates blockers when evidence records omit required scope field.
evidence.why: Validation prevents ambiguous evidence sources.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns explorer, cross-checker, verifier for cross_check policy to force independent verification.
evidence.why: Cross-check protocol prevents single-agent errors on high-risk claims.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, with explicit BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves boundary audit enforcement works.

evidence.summary: Tests verify external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts and scope-less evidence is blocked.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves evidence scope enforcement prevents unverified claims.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false.
evidence.why: Protocol contract requires evidence agents to be read-only.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs Claude/codex/custom provider commands with configurable tool profiles for evidence agent execution.
evidence.why: Provider abstraction enables full-tool or boundary-restricted agent spawning.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt executes providers with timeout, captures stdout/stderr, and detects budget exceeded failures.
evidence.why: Provider runtime handles evidence agent execution and failure classification.

risks: If boundary snapshot logic fails to detect mutations in non-git repos, agents could corrupt projects without blockers.
If claude provider ignores tool profile restrictions, full-tool agents might mutate projects despite boundary policy.
If evidence scope classification misclassifies external URLs as project, unverified claims could become facts.

result.notes: Evidence confirms boundary audit exists, is tested, and blocks PRD readiness on mutations or missing scope. Cross-check protocol forces independent verification for high-risk claims.

agent_results.claim: Independent verification confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that blocks PRD readiness when full-tool Claude agents mutate target project files or lack required evidence scope declarations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot walks project files, computes digests, excludes allowed roots; diffBoundarySnapshots detects added/modified/deleted files by comparing digest maps.
evidence.why: Confirmed boundary detection logic matches explorer claim exactly.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot taken before agents, diff after execution; boundaryChanges length determines violated vs clean; changes array records mutations.
evidence.why: Verified runtime enforcement flow matches claimed implementation.

evidence.summary: execution_policy explicitly sets project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots to output_dir only.
evidence.why: Confirmed policy documentation of boundary contract.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rules explicitly forbid modifying target project files and require evidence scope declarations.
evidence.why: Verified evidence agent instructions match claimed restrictions.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set restricts evidence to project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Confirmed scope validation set matches explorer claim.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence by scope and source type; project facts require project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Verified classification logic enforces evidence requirements.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with concrete locators or have non-project scopes only.
evidence.why: Confirmed blocking logic prevents unverified claims.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues generates blockers when evidence records omit required scope field.
evidence.why: Verified validation prevents ambiguous evidence sources.

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass; explicit BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker present.
evidence.why: Confirmed test proves boundary audit enforcement works.

evidence.summary: Tests verify external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts and scope-less evidence is blocked.
evidence.why: Verified test evidence proves scope enforcement prevents unverified claims.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false.
evidence.why: Confirmed protocol contract requires evidence agents to be read-only.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs Claude/codex/custom provider commands with configurable tool profiles for evidence agent execution.
evidence.why: Verified provider abstraction enables full-tool agent spawning.

risks: Boundary snapshot digest computation may not detect all mutation types (e.g., metadata-only changes on systems with coarse mtime).
If claude provider ignores tool profile restrictions at runtime, full-tool agents could mutate projects despite boundary policy declaration.
Evidence scope classification heuristics could misclassify ambiguous sources, though project path presence provides strong signal.

result.notes: All explorer claims independently verified via direct source reads. Line numbers, function names, logic flows, and test assertions match exactly. No conflicts found. Boundary audit implementation is confirmed to exist, be tested, and block PRD readiness on mutations or missing scope.

agent_results.claim: Independent verification confirms: gathered evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness acceptance criteria. Explorer and cross-checker provided project-scoped evidence with concrete paths, no assumptions presented as facts, no unresolved missing data, and boundary audit enforcement is verified by both code and tests.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker independently confirmed buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots detect file mutations via digest maps.
evidence.why: Two independent agents verified same implementation with matching line numbers.

evidence.summary: Both agents verified runtime boundary enforcement: snapshot before agents, diff after, violations generate blockers.
evidence.why: Cross-check confirms exact logic flow without conflict.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker verified test confirms BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker prevents PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Test evidence independently verified by both agents.

evidence.summary: Both agents verified projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Cross-check confirmed enforcement prevents unverified claims.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker verified tests prove external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts.
evidence.why: Test evidence independently verified.

risks: If provider adapter spawns full-tool agents that ignore tool profile restrictions, boundary audit could be bypassed at runtime despite policy declaration.
If evidence scope classification has edge cases where project-scoped sources are misclassified as external, valid evidence could be rejected.

result.why: Explorer and cross-checker provided consistent, independent verification of all claims with project-scoped evidence from code and tests. No conflicts, no assumptions presented as facts, no unresolved missing data. Acceptance criteria met: all agents cite project paths, boundary audit blocks mutations, PRD readiness requires valid evidence.
result.notes: Verifier confirms evidence agreement status is pass with zero conflicts. All 13 evidence records have project scope with concrete paths. 2 tests confirm enforcement. PRD readiness acceptance criteria satisfied.
